feed.title: 8holic
feed.subtitle: The main hub by 8holic (Real name Bryan) to create a hub to link all existing projects and files.

entry.title: Reading: Hoshi Ori Yume Mirai - Sora Route

link.title: Reading: Hoshi Ori Yume Mirai - Sora Route

entry.content:
Synopsis

The novel covers Hino Ryousuke returning to his hometown of Shionagi and his school life. It also cover his struggles as well as romance with Ousaka Sora, the 1st year astrology club president and its only member. The first part focus on the incoming Tanabata festival and the stories while preparing for it, with a focus on Ousaka-san and her growth. The second part focus more on Hino-san and his career inspirations as he is starting out his new firm as an architect.

First Impressions

The first thing I noticed is that most of the characters have a obession that actually demonstrates. Ousaka-san knowledge of astrology actually go beyond common knowledge and I learnt quite a few things about constellations. Intresting trivia like less light pollution(obvious) and dry air(not obvious) is better for stargazing, or how Zeus is connected to yes amount of constellations. This stands out to me as a lot of anime or visual novel usually tell us that a character is good but never demonstrate knowledge beyond what literally anyone will know. It enforces Ousaka-san as interested in astrology and she is someone I can see asking about this topic.

Stand out moments

To start out with the most obvious thing Hino-san is an actual character and not a self insert. He has his own goals and is seeking out an internship at the start. We see that he is overly confident and humbled by Mo-san(I can’t remember his name but Ousaka-san calls him this) who is someone he look up to. He is someone gentle and care about his girlfriend and how she is feeling, and he struggles with coming up with a correct solution. I felt that being able to describe Hino-san this ways instantly makes him more intresting and I can actually see why…well Ousaka-san in this case falls for him.

The confession scene was notable because it occurs at what I will consider a moment of weakness of the protagonist. To give context even after Hino-san realize that he has fallen for Ousaka-san, he is determined to confess only AFTER the festial so that if he is rejected it will not affect his duties(which is to convince Ousaka-san to participate in the festival). However after seeing that Ousaka-san is popular and have suitors, when they are together he went ahead with the confession anyways. He even realizes that this can end poorly.

Ousaka-san also does not respond immediately and request time to consider, despite this the relationship afterwards did not become akward. Hino-San even explained the reason he isn’t avoiding her while giving her time to think about it. So when Ousaka-san give her response, even if I already know the answer it is a sweet moment.

Throughout the route, constellations are an important part of the relationship beyond a interest, the protagonist reference Zeus and constellations as they kiss for the first time, additionally the final location they are seen together is referenced very early on when they are studnets as the “ideal stargazing spot”.

The sex scenes are quite sweet as well and it reveals another side of the charcater, Ousaka-san is notably more assertive in this segment while Hino-san is really careful throughout the relationship. It helps establish the relationship with each other. Notably unlike quite a few porngraphic material Ousaka-san felt like an actual person instead of a prop to arouse the user.

As of the challanges most of them are relatively realistic and things that can actually happen in real life (such as the rain ruining some of the cardboard storage).

Highlight of the story

The most notable part of the story is the adult route when Hino-San open his firm and is stressed out by multiple vectors, with many things to do. He actually suffered the consequences of this excessive load and fainted in office, coming to a tearful Ousaka-san. Whereas they have talk about marriage previously, the marriage proposal from Ousaka-san and the sacrifice she will make is what cause Hino-San to realize how badly he has screwed up. What he said afterwards was one of my favorite parts where he takes responsbility and take care of himself for both his and Ousaka-san sake.

Complaints

I know I litearlly just praised the sex scene earlier, but I felt that they might be too stacked with each other. I primary take issues with the back to back sex scenes, it kinda kill the pacing. I think the offending scene is after the second time they have sex, they go for round 2 in the shower agian. I was kinda waiting for them to get on with the normal interaction. If it is spread out a little more I will not have an issue.

Closing thoughts

TWhile I am probably bias because Ousaka-san is exactly my type of girl, I felt that the route is very sweet throughout in showing the relationship as well as the struggles. My favorite part is that the focus is split on both the protagonist and the love interest.
entry.summary: Synopsis The novel covers Hino Ryousuke returning to his hometown of Shionagi and his school life. It also cover his struggles as well as romance with Ousaka Sora, the 1st year astrology club president and its only member. The first part focus on the incoming Tanabata festival and the stories while preparing for it, with a focus on Ousaka-san and her growth. The second part focus more on Hino-san and his career inspirations as he is starting out his new firm as an architect.

entry.title: Reading: Atomic Habits
entry.content:
Background

I was reading the book Atomic Habits and I was reading the first few pages. I found what was said to be insightful, in determining what I want to be instead of the end goal. I find that usually when my parents ask me to clean my room usually it get messy again soon enough. Reading this made me realize that it is purely because the habits do not substain the goal. So I wish to reflect on this. Starting with the below

What type of person do I want to be?

This question is suprising difficult to answer, despite being a seemingly easy question. I broke it down into the following questions to answer instead.

What am I doing NOW

-Going to internship -Preparing for University -Play video games -Running my Toastmaster Club -Helping my community network in tech areas -Mindlessly browsing and listening to Music -Cleaning up my room

What will I CONTINUE doing/STOP doing?

What I want to do now What I will stop doing
Studying OSINT/tech related things Going to University

I was a little suprise to see that I listed both studying and University. I listed the two and I think I see the issue

Aspect University Education Self Study (OSINT/tech)
Pace Fixed, often rushed Self‑paced, can slow down to understand
Teaching style Professors focused on research, not teaching Self‑selected resources, learn by doing
Motivation Studying for tests, external pressure Internal curiosity, goals I set myself
Goal setting Curriculum‑driven, rigid I set the study goals and path
Schedule control Fixed class times, attendance requirements I decide when, how long, and the rhythm
Autonomy over content Core subjects, prerequisites, limited choice I choose exactly what I want, in any order
Evaluation pressure Grades, exams, GPA, bell curve Self‑assessment, pulbic perception
Hierarchy Professors as authority, power distance Bad resources get discarded
Intellectual freedom Curriculum decides what’s relevant I get to chase after useless knowledge

I did not realize these restriction until I head to University Charles III of Madrid for an exchange semester, where the way lessosn are conducted there affected my thoughts. The key difference is the the professors are more open to questions and meeting with students, as well as more intellectual freedom to explore topics we are interested in(even if my group squandered it). I think this is why it is more toleratable even through it is signficantly more difficult. I want to solve the ‘how’ and not go through a rigid process.

Based on this, here is my first identity: I want to be a student of technology.

What I want to do now What I will stop doing
Travel overseas Mindlessly Browse the web
Do things with intent Lose time to Listening to Music
Finish reading my bookshelf Stop buying more books
Help uplift the community Eat unhealth sugarly drinks
Go for Health Checkup  

These are group together because they have a common theme, which is active vs passive consumption. The left side are about doing and experiencing things, while most of the thing I want to stop are passive consumptions. For things on the left I felt that they are usually harder to start, however upon starting it is harder to stop. Whereas things on the right are easy to start and easy to stay engaged, but at the end I felt that I haven’t done anything.

So the second Identity will be: I want to be someone who does and experience.
entry.summary: Background I was reading the book Atomic Habits and I was reading the first few pages. I found what was said to be insightful, in determining what I want to be instead of the end goal. I find that usually when my parents ask me to clean my room usually it get messy again soon enough. Reading this made me realize that it is purely because the habits do not substain the goal. So I wish to reflect on this. Starting with the below What type of person do I want to be? This question is suprising difficult to answer, despite being a seemingly easy question. I broke it down into the following questions to answer instead. What am I doing NOW -Going to internship -Preparing for University -Play video games -Running my Toastmaster Club -Helping my community network in tech areas -Mindlessly browsing and listening to Music -Cleaning up my room What will I CONTINUE doing/STOP doing?

entry.title: Limbus Company ID Visualization
entry.content:
Visualization

Ideation

I heard people mention a few stats like certain character having the longest wait, so I was always kinda curious. I decide to take this own to use some of the things I learnt last semester.

getting the data point.

Since I already know there is a data point, I start off by checking robot.txt to make sure that they are ok with it. I noticed they block a lot of AI agents, and some spider. So I decide to use Selenium. The idea I had was that I would first get the URL, then I will run it through to grab certain information. Most of the challanges are things I partially remember how to deal with, such as tabs and simillar things. I set my agent to have a 1 second delay on every agent just to ensure I do not DDOS the server by accident. Overall it went pretty ok, but these are the challanges.

  1. Navigating the differnt character This one was pretty ok, I realize very early on that the ID divison goes up by 2 instead of 1 for ID, so I ran two loops, the first loop will run the internal ID, the second loop oversee the entire thing to change character. We get the url here.
  2. Inconsistent format Among the older ID, sometimes the date is just slightly enough that the XPath misses it, I think there is an extra divison somewhere because this is the difference. I end up doing a either/or for certain parameters, however this is not that significant and I corrected some of them manually. For the major one like skills, I use a fuzzy form of searching instead, searching for Skill1 is enough compared to Skill1_0
  3. All the weird characters Something intresting, but excel cried pretty badly when processing korean/japanese/chinese characters. Installed the language pack to make this work out. I also wanted to get the element affilation even through I didn’t do anything about it, after looking around I decide that I can simply grab the name of the png, which is usually used to visually tell a reader.

But all in all, the data was relatively easy to get due to the relatively standalized format, it is not super clean like the perfect data my school gave me, but it is a little challanging to get. Most of the challange come from navigating the site.

Cleaning the data was intresting as well, however I will cover this more in the next part.

Visualizing the data

I learnt a bit about google data studio, so over here I decide to try my hand. Importing the data isn’t too difficult, however I soon realize that this is the hardest segment Challanges faced

  1. Getting the X axis to be a time series Upon going in I already have a pretty good idea what I want the graph to look like, which is an issue when all the X axis generally support metrics only and not dates. Time series results in a line which isn’t exactly suited for these 1 off data. Initally I thought to use the UEIX format for date in order to display as metrics, however due to the large value, it ends up really skewing the graph making it hard to read. In the end I just used a community visuzliaer instead. Thank you supermetrics
  2. Making the data format correct It become very clear that 19.02.2026 is being registered as a text, which is an issue, this wasn’t too bad as I used power query(or the google equivalent) to convert it into a date format. It took a while to get the formula right but it was pretty ok
  3. Y axis must be a metrics By far the largest issue I spend on, since I am using the names of the character, I basically have 12 strings while they only accept numbers. I looked around a bit, and eventurally decide I should convert the names into number so I can plot the graph. This leads to the final but most vexxing issue
  4. The legends order is wrong Since the previous segment is the Y axis, and the numbers don’t exactly mean anything, we will need someway to read the data. Initally I thought since our name has numbers, we will sort via numerical order, however the order seems to be random. Initally I thought it was alphabetical order, so I ran the parameter to add a number in front of the sinner names…and nothing changed. It isn’t numerical either since when I change the number everything jumped. I think it has something to do with the sort tab but I fiddle with it and nothing change. I have no idea what was causing this and was getting a little fustrated, so I decide perfection is the enemy of good. So I just published it on reddit: https://www.reddit.com/r/limbuscompany/comments/1r8y6iv/as_limbus_enter_its_third_year_i_tried/ As of writing I literally just publish it so I am unsure of the reception. However I believe I have reach a good end point.

Conclusion

Overall I learnt a bit about data visualization and how finnicky the software can be at competiting against what I wanted. I also learnt a bit about using creative walkarounds, through the final issue is still unresolved. I also realize how scary it is to publish things I have done online even if fundamentally it doesn’t matter, I am unsure why? Maybe it is the fear of someone saying something mean about it, this would be an area of growth.

entry.title: Pre Exchange Experience-Day 1
entry.content:
Pre Exchange Experience - Italy Tourism Day 1

Background

As a student heading for exchange, I decided to do a trip in europe since I haven’t really travel outside of Asia. I decided on Italy due to its relative close proximity to my exchange university in Spain.

Milan - Standout Moments

First impressions

Upon arrival the first thing I noticed is that the buildings seems older. There isn’t lots of skyscraper, most buildings are quite old

Duomo di Milano

Milan’s Cathedral was the first location I visited, it looks pretty old. I really enjoyed looking at the various status around it, going inside I see the pillars and decorations. It is the first time I am seeing such a detailed cathedral.

The interior is very spacious. I sat down and just enjoyed the enviroment, it is very quiet.

I climbed to the rooftop,it was a tough climb. A few hundred steps, I didn’t take the elevator. The view of the city is nice, it feels like a older city.

Wandering Around

I came across this layout of the city, and it is suprising. I have never seen this before, it feels intresting to see these maps and think of why they were built in the past

The next thing I noticed is the existance of these water fountain. In Singapore these are rare and inconvenient, I cannot believe that these are just out here. I drank a bit and the water was really refreshing.

Museo del Duomo di Milano

In the museum they talk about the history of the cathedral, it is quite intesting. I saw how they built the cathedral up over time. I went to a few museum so I am just going to group them together, the above image is that of a clock. It has always just worked I have never thought about how such a simple clock works all the time.

This was a pretty creepy underground area, It was an intresting experience walking underground.

Final thoughts

The first day impression started with a robbery on the train, but overall it was pretty exciting. I felt thrusted in a foreign world, everything is new and exciting.

Through the most challanging part was when the hotel cancel on me as i board the flight, booking on the plane causes me a bit of anxiety but it is good it works out.

entry.title: URECA Project Presentation
entry.content:
Overview

Last year I took part in the Undergraduate Research Experience on Campus(URECA) Project. I was tasked with figuring our a way to port an experience to a more consumer friendly version. The photo above was my presentation during the URECA Conference. Overall this was a wonderful 1 year where I learnt a lot. I was supervised by Assoc Professor Benjamin.

I felt that the professor was wonderful, it was mostly unsupervised due to his busy schedule. This gave me a lot of opporunity to mostly figure things out by myself and decide on my course of action, with inputs from him during each presentation.

The things I done well is the following: -The ability to think creatively to solve issues -The ability to work consistenly on the project throughout the semester -The ability to present my work to the professor -The conclusion of the project involved it being presented to people in the community center after the above conference, I think it is a good showcase of the work to a wider audiance.

The things I think I can do better: -Clearer communication or more meaningful communiation to the professor, I am unsure if the update aren’t significant enough or he is busy but sometimes I am not sure how to proceed. Usually I can just figure something out, but at the end when it comes to publishing rights I am not sure if I should do it under my account or under the school since the school owns all the project -Preseting a demo, some of my presentation did not work when presenting to the professor and I believe he had some trouble to get it to work properly.

I think that some factors are out of control as I am not very sure about the school proceedings, NTU being a black box does not help either. While I was unable to publish the work, I hope that I have done enough that the Professor will get there eventurally.
entry.summary: Overview Last year I took part in the Undergraduate Research Experience on Campus(URECA) Project. I was tasked with figuring our a way to port an experience to a more consumer friendly version. The photo above was my presentation during the URECA Conference. Overall this was a wonderful 1 year where I learnt a lot. I was supervised by Assoc Professor Benjamin.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 6&7
entry.content:
Overview

So I realize I do not have much to write here, so I combined both days

Wakayama

Intent

Hot Spring are kinda what is popular in Japan, so I went to this hot spring resort.

Experience

As I am aware there is no food I bought some stuff before I went. It was an intresting experience as I was given something to wear in the hotel. My room was facing the ocean. I went for the hot spring and they list the composition(in japanese), soaking inside I can feel like something is on my skin. I went twice, once at night and another in the morning.

In the morning I was at the outdoors section where I can look at the nearby highway. After that I had breakfast which was a spread of random meals. I walked around Kada town a bit before departing.

Memory

The Hot Spring certainly feel nice, it is an unique experience. It was relatively isolated since I arrive late/early respectively.

Insight

I need to stay for more days in the future, one day isn’t enough unfortuantely.

Reflection - Overall

All in all it was an enjoyable trip, however the last few days are hampered by poor planning making them less memorable compared to the first few days. I hope to do this again in the future.
entry.summary: Overview So I realize I do not have much to write here, so I combined both days

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 5
entry.content:
Kyoto, Fushimi Inari Shrine

Intent

To be honest I was trying to avoid the crowd, I thought it will be cool since it will be late at night.

Experience

I went down the shrine at 12AM basically, the entire place feels calming initally. However around the Tori gates the atmosphere become very opressive, espeically as I can hear the wild boars and friends. I did not finish the ascend as planned and I dipped out midway through.

Memory

Something about the night turn a simple walk into a scary one. I think it is the lack of people or the darkness, however the number of Tori gate make the experience a surreal one

Insight

I hope to go again in the morning, through I learnt the importance of pulling out. The entire experience was an unique one but I am unsure if my heart can handle this.

Kyoto, Railway Museum and Tower

Intent

I didn’t have any real goal in mind so I was just googling for intresting things.

Experience/Memory

To be honest it is pretty forgettable, the only thing I rembered is that the fog blocked the view. Oh yes and there is a minature section to drive trains.

Insights

Maybe spamming random activites and commiting to it isn’t the best idea if I clearly don’t care about it.

Kyoto, Gear Play

Intent

I saw the advertisement as a “wordless” play, which is something I think I can enjoy even without a translator so I went inside.

Experience

I sat down and we watched a play, the entire play features 5 character and a set. The production is really high and there are flyers and stuff. I took a few home.

Memory

This was the highlight of the kyoto trip suprising, the entire play was something that convey emotion despite not a single word being spoken. They conveyed the sense of creativity and things quite well, as well as using creative stageplay. The emotions conveyed are really strong, I even bought the stageplay and some shirts. Even if it is all in Japanese so I need to get on with learning the language to read it.

Insight

It seems I had some misconception on plays on account of only being exposed to my school terrible plays. In the future I will seek our more theatres to watch and expand my horizon.

Insight-Overall

I learnt the plan is to plan more time. 1 day in Kyoto is insufficient and one of the main issue I ran into is the fact that I am constrained by time, some attractions and museums are not open that day so I wasn’t able to visit.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 4
entry.content:
Ikoma Sanjo Amusement Park

Intent

An amusement park on the hill was something I was curious about, the main drive is curosity.

Experience

I went up via a train, the amusement park is free to visit. Payment is only for specific rides. I went to the flying plane as well as the “White House”. Afterwards I climbed down the hill and walked back to the train.

Memory

The trains are very cute and have a nice design. The flying plane is great because I was able to see both Nara and Osaka from a high overview. Additionally the White house depicted an intresting version of America, the building is tilted by a few degrees. The founding myth was shown as a bridge, then followed by a steam train. Notably the train noise is clearly a vaccum cleaner. At the end, lego was used to depict the moon landing.

Insight

Overall the entire place was really enjoyable despite the low budget vibe. Some of the creative solutions are something to inspire me. In the future I learn how the budget does not determine the quality.

Nara, Deer Park

Intent

The Nara Park are kinda popular and I heard of them before.

Experience

There are indeed, many deers. I bought some deer biscuit but a single deer forcefully ripped it from my fingers. Additionally I visited the nearby shrines, and sat down on a bench and watch the deers just laze around.

Memory

The sheer number of deers is certainly something to remember, through being robbed by a deer was not expected. Sitting on the bench was calming and relaxing.

Insight

I guess I learnt how scary wild animals can be, and also how calming is it to do nothing. I wish to come back to stare at the deers in the future.
